title: thymus serpyllum subsp. jankae
intro: Thymus serpyllum subsp. jankae, also known as Jankae Thyme, is a subspecies of the thyme plant, a low-growing, flowering perennial herb native to the Balkan Peninsula. Thymus serpyllum subsp. jankae has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its antimicrobial, antifungal, and anti-inflammatory effects, which are attributed to the presence of essential oils such as thymol and carvacrol. The health benefits of Jankae Thyme include its ability to boost the immune system, reduce stress and anxiety, and alleviate respiratory issues such as coughs and colds. Thymus serpyllum subsp. jankae can be prepared as an herbal remedy through infusions, decoctions, or essential oil distillation, and is often used in traditional medicine to support overall well-being.
